title: Scandinavian Defense
shortDescription: A direct reply to 1.e4 with 1...d5, trading the center pawn immediately for quick, simplified development.

children.comment: The queen retreats while remaining active.
A useful move supporting ...Bf5 and giving the queen c7.

line.name: Main Line: 3...Qa5
line.description: The classical Scandinavian setup with ...c6, ...Bf5 and ...e6.

line.name: 3...Qa5: Classical Main Line
line.description: A central theoretical setup with active bishop development.

line.name: 3...Qa5: Deep Main Line
line.description: A deeper classical sequence with structural imbalance after a knight exchange.

line.name: 3...Qa5: Queen Recapture Structure
line.description: Black accepts queen activity and the bishop pair after Nxf6+.

line.name: 3...Qa5: Kingside Expansion
line.description: White gains space with g4; Black preserves the bishop.

line.name: 3...Qa5: Ne5 Jump
line.description: White centralizes a knight; Black develops and challenges the center.

line.name: 3...Qa5: Bd2 Setup
line.description: White prepares long castling; Black develops in standard fashion.

line.name: 3...Qa5: Long-Castling Setup
line.description: White castles queenside and Black develops toward a sharp middlegame.

line.name: 3...Qa5: Opposite-Side Castling
line.description: A sharp setup where both sides may launch pawn storms.

line.name: 3...Qa5: Early Nf3
line.description: A natural development line leading to the standard Scandinavian structure.

line.name: 3...Qa5: Early Bc4
line.description: Black answers quick development with ...Nf6 and ...c6.

line.name: Main Line: 3...Qd8
line.description: A safe queen retreat followed by simple development.

line.name: 3...Qd8: Classical Structure
line.description: A solid setup with ...Nf6, ...c6, ...Bf5 and ...e6.

line.name: 3...Qd8: Bf4 Development
line.description: White develops the dark bishop early; Black uses a compact setup.

line.name: Main Line: 3...Qd6
line.description: The queen stays active and supports a later ...e5 break.

line.name: 3...Qd6: Central ...e5 Break
line.description: Black prepares a direct central counterstrike.

line.name: 3...Qd6: Main Development
line.description: Black keeps the queen centralized and develops compactly.

line.name: 3...Qd6: Fianchetto Setup
line.description: White fianchettos while Black prepares a central break.

line.name: Modern Scandinavian: 3.d4
line.description: Black delays the recapture and develops with tempo.

line.name: Modern Scandinavian: Knight Recapture
line.description: Black recaptures with the knight and prepares a kingside fianchetto.

line.name: Modern Scandinavian: Fianchetto Main Line
line.description: Black combines a knight recapture with kingside fianchetto development.

line.name: Portuguese Variation: 3...Bg4
line.description: Black values rapid development and pressure over immediate pawn recovery.

line.name: Portuguese Variation: Sharp Main Line
line.description: Black obtains rapid activity for delayed material recovery.

line.name: Portuguese Variation: 4.Be2
line.description: White avoids weakening the kingside; Black recaptures and develops.

line.name: Portuguese Variation: 4.Nf3
line.description: Black recaptures with the queen and keeps active piece play.

line.name: Modern Scandinavian: 3.Nf3
line.description: Black regains the pawn with the knight and prepares a fianchetto.

line.name: Modern Scandinavian: 3.c4
line.description: White tries to keep the pawn; Black undermines it with ...c6.

line.name: Blackburne-Kloosterboer Gambit
line.description: Black uses ...c6 to undermine White’s extra pawn.

line.name: Modern Scandinavian: 3.c4 Main Line
line.description: Black undermines the pawn chain and gains active central play.

line.name: Icelandic Gambit
line.description: Black offers a pawn for development and open lines.

line.name: Icelandic Gambit: Main Line
line.description: Black develops with tempo and castles queenside for attacking chances.

line.name: Modern Scandinavian: 3.Bb5+
line.description: Black blocks the check and attacks the extra pawn with development.

line.name: Modern Scandinavian: 3.Bb5+ Deep Line
line.description: Black neutralizes the check and develops aggressively.

line.name: Scandinavian Declined: 2.e5
line.description: Black attacks the advanced center with ...c5.
